front: How do you read and write Iceberg tables with Apache Spark — what catalog configuration is needed?
back: Iceberg integrates with Spark via the iceberg-spark-runtime JAR and catalog configuration in SparkSession. Configure a catalog: `spark.sql.catalog.my_catalog = org.apache.iceberg.spark.SparkCatalog` and `spark.sql.catalog.my_catalog.type = hadoop` (for S3/HDFS) or `rest` for REST catalog. Writing: `df.writeTo('my_catalog.db.my_table').using('iceberg').createOrReplace()` or via SQL `INSERT INTO my_catalog.db.my_table SELECT ...`. Reading: `spark.table('my_catalog.db.my_table')` or `spark.read.format('iceberg').load('s3://bucket/warehouse/db/my_table')`. Upserts: `df.createOrReplaceTempView('updates'); spark.sql('MERGE INTO my_catalog.db.target t USING updates u ON t.id = u.id WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET * WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT *')`. Time travel: `spark.read.option('snapshot-id', '12345').format('iceberg').load(...)`. The Hadoop catalog is file-system based (no external service); production systems should use Hive Metastore or REST catalog for concurrent writes and catalog management.
